Class TaskContext

java.lang.Object
com.cmw.adaptive.task.TaskContext
public final class TaskContext extends Object
Encapsulates execution metadata for a task, including timestamps for submission, start, and completion.

Used internally by the executor to track task lifecycle and calculate metrics such as queue wait time and execution duration.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    TaskContext()
    Creates a new TaskContext with the submission timestamp set to the current time.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    long
    completedAtNanos()
    Returns the timestamp when the task was marked as completed.
    long
    executionTimeNanos()
    Time taken to execute the task.
    void
    markCompleted()
    Marks the task as completed by recording the completion timestamp.
    void
    markStarted()
    Marks the task as started by recording the start timestamp.
    long
    queueWaitTimeNanos()
    Time spent waiting in the queue before execution started.
    long
    startedAtNanos()
    Returns the timestamp when the task was marked as started.
    long
    submittedAtNanos()
    Returns the timestamp when the task was submitted to the executor.
    long
    totalLatencyNanos()
    Total time from submission to completion.

  • Constructor Details

    • TaskContext

      public TaskContext()
      Creates a new TaskContext with the submission timestamp set to the current time.

  • Method Details

    • submittedAtNanos

      public long submittedAtNanos()
      Returns the timestamp when the task was submitted to the executor.
      Returns:
      submission timestamp in nanoseconds

    • startedAtNanos

      public long startedAtNanos()
      Returns the timestamp when the task was marked as started.
      Returns:
      start timestamp in nanoseconds, or 0 if not started

    • completedAtNanos

      public long completedAtNanos()
      Returns the timestamp when the task was marked as completed.
      Returns:
      completion timestamp in nanoseconds, or 0 if not completed

    • markStarted

      public void markStarted()
      Marks the task as started by recording the start timestamp.

    • markCompleted

      public void markCompleted()
      Marks the task as completed by recording the completion timestamp.

    • queueWaitTimeNanos

      public long queueWaitTimeNanos()
      Time spent waiting in the queue before execution started.
      Returns:
      queue wait time in nanoseconds, or 0 if not started

    • executionTimeNanos

      public long executionTimeNanos()
      Time taken to execute the task.
      Returns:
      execution time in nanoseconds, or 0 if not completed

    • totalLatencyNanos

      public long totalLatencyNanos()
      Total time from submission to completion.
      Returns:
      total latency in nanoseconds, or 0 if not completed
